weather.summary: Saturday: 9°C, light rain. Sunday: 9°C, broken clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend offers a fantastic mix of indoor and outdoor activities perfect for a curious 3-year-old! From free museum visits with hands-on exhibits to exciting theatre performances, there's something for everyone, no matter the weather. I'm especially excited about the Young V&A's 'Play Saturdays' and the Easter events starting to pop up!

activities.title: Play Saturdays (Jan-Mar 2026)
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Drop-in play sessions at the Young V&A museum. Explore the museum's collection and get creative.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on activities, space to move, and engaging exhibits designed for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green. The museum is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Check the Young V&A website for specific themes and activities.

activities.title: SEND Play - Feb-June 2026
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Drop-in sessions designed for children with Special Educational Needs and Disabilities.
activities.whyItWorks: Sensory-friendly environment, adapted activities, and a welcoming space for all children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green. The museum is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Check the Young V&A website for specific dates and times.

activities.title: Library Club
activities.description: With reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: Good way to encourage reading

transport.tip: Check Hackney Showroom website for details

activities.title: Have some family fun at the Young V&A Museum
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Get crafty at the origami workshop, learn how to design collages and more.
activities.whyItWorks: interactive displays, workshops and an Open Studio, and show off performance skills on the amphitheatre-style stage

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Located a short walk from Bethnal Green tube station

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm
activities.description: free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts.
activities.whyItWorks: Families and animal-lovers, with a host of goats, sheep and smaller animals

activities.title: Visit a London city farm
activities.description: See if you can spot some spring arrivals.
activities.whyItWorks: See animals

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Free Zoo
activities.whyItWorks: See animals

transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in the park

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the museum's extensive collections of natural history specimens.
activities.whyItWorks: The dinosaurs and other animal exhibits are sure to capture a 3-year-old's imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Underground (Piccadilly Line) to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Booking a free ticket in advance is recommended.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Easter Hunt
activities.description: Explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: An Easter trail that actually motivates kids to walk. You'll explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat

transport.fromHackney: Train from Waterloo to Hampton Court

activities.title: ZSL London Zoo: The Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: An Easter trail with fun facts about egg-laying animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animals and an Easter theme, making it engaging for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Camden Road, then a bus to London Zoo.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance to save time.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Follow the trail, matching the names of influential characters to the Lindt gold bunny statues and collecting a mini chocolate treat when you've found them all.
activities.whyItWorks: Good way to explore the gardens

transport.fromHackney: Train from Waterloo to Hampton Court

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.price: Free with entry to the ship
activities.description: Take part in an Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship.
activities.whyItWorks: Discover fascinating facts about Cutty Sark's epic voyages

transport.tip: Suitable for young explorers age five+ and their families.

activities.title: Hall Place & Gardens – Easter Celebrations
activities.description: Explore Hall Place's historic house and gardens, with Easter egg hunts, trails, and workshops.
activities.whyItWorks: Seasonal blooms and family-friendly events create an engaging day out for visitors and students.

activities.title: Legoland Windsor Easter Activities
activities.description: Easter activities with rides and attractions to explore, plus brick building challenges, workshops, and character meet ups.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for LEGO fans

activities.title: London Zoo – Zoo-normous Easter Hunt
activities.description: The London Zoo hosts an Easter hunt featuring animal-themed challenges.
activities.whyItWorks: Children can follow trails to discover hidden eggs while learning fun facts about wildlife.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Camden Town then bus

activities.title: Wimbledon Quarter Easter Egg Hunt
activities.description: Easter Egg Hunt.
activities.whyItWorks: fun for the whole family

activities.title: Mini Makers: Messy Play
activities.description: Messy play sessions.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of messy fun

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: theatre production
activities.whyItWorks: Classic story

activities.title: Family Easter Makes Workshop at Kenwood
activities.description: Easter themed workshop
activities.whyItWorks: Get creative

activities.title: Easter Trail
activities.description: Easter Trail
activities.whyItWorks: Easter Fun

activities.title: Tower Bridge Easter Family Activities
activities.dates: Tuesday 31 March, Tuesday 7 April and Saturday 11 April 2026
activities.price: Included in your visit to Tower Bridge.
activities.description: Designed for curious minds and energetic explorers, Tower Bridge's Easter Family Activities are free with admission and combine making, movement and mechanical marvels, giving children the chance to discover how Victorian engineers and inventors shaped the modern world, all while exploring the Bridge from top to bottom.
activities.whyItWorks: making, movement and mechanical marvels

transport.tip: Located just outside the City of London boundary

activities.title: The Lion King
activities.description: London's West End hosts spectacular musicals and plays, perfect for Easter entertainment.
activities.whyItWorks: Family-friendly shows like Matilda and The Lion King offer immersive performances, colorful sets, and live music.

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: Heartwarming and hilarious adventure.
activities.whyItWorks: Show

activities.title: Playing Shakespeare with Deutsche Bank: Romeo and Juliet
activities.description: Critically acclaimed 90-min version created especially for young people.
activities.whyItWorks: Enjoyable performance
